name: Emergency Responders
description_short: Emergency Responders Credit Union is a North Carolina-based credit union offering deposit products, savings accounts, and personal loans to members.
description_long: Emergency Responders Credit Union is a federally-chartered credit union located in Winston-Salem, North Carolina at 1777 Link Road. Based on MonitorBankRates' evaluation, the institution serves members seeking traditional credit union banking services with a focus on accessibility and competitive rates. The credit union offers a range of deposit products including certificates of deposit (CDs) at various terms and balances, savings accounts, and money market accounts, along with personal lending services. Their CD rates range from 3.25% to 4.00% depending on term length and deposit amount, with separate JUMBO CD rates for deposits of $30,000 or more. Emergency Responders Credit Union maintains financial stability with a B+ Overall Health Grade and a 4.8 out of 5 star rating from MonitorBankRates, based on NCUA regulatory data. The institution's Texas Ratio of 17.88% is higher than the national average of 6.13%, indicating some non-performing assets but with sufficient capital reserves. As a credit union, membership requirements likely apply, though specific eligibility criteria are not detailed on this review page. The credit union's personal loan rates are competitive at 9.00% APR, making it an option for members seeking credit-union-sourced financing.
services: 12-month JUMBO Certificates of Deposit at 3.75% APY ($30,000 minimum)
24-month JUMBO Certificates of Deposit at 3.75% APY ($30,000 minimum)
36-month JUMBO Certificates of Deposit at 3.50% APY ($30,000 minimum)
pros: 4.8 out of 5 star rating (Excellent) from MonitorBankRates with B+ health grade
18-month CD rate of 4.00% competitive for standard deposits ($500 minimum)
JUMBO CD rates at 3.75% for 12 and 24-month terms ($30,000 minimum)
Money market account rates at 1.00% available for liquidity-focused savers
Personal loan rates at 9.00% APR provide credit union lending option
Strong capitalization with sufficient reserves relative to non-performing assets
Multiple CD term options (12, 18, 24, and 36 months) for flexible savings strategies
cons: Texas Ratio of 17.88% is significantly higher than the national average of 6.13%, indicating elevated non-performing assets
Savings account rates of 0.25% are extremely low and unlikely to outpace inflation
Limited information provided about membership eligibility requirements and qualifications
Personal loan rate of 9.00% may not be competitive compared to other credit unions or banks
No details provided about checking accounts, online banking features, or mobile app functionality
best_for: Current credit union members seeking certificate of deposit products with multi-year terms
Consumers wanting to consolidate savings across multiple CD terms and JUMBO deposit tiers
Individuals needing personal loans through a credit union lender with moderate APR
Members prioritizing federally-insured deposit accounts over higher-yield alternatives
diagnosis: Emergency Responders Credit Union is best for existing credit union members seeking federally-insured savings products with modest yields and those needing personal credit union loans. The main caveat is the institution's Texas Ratio of 17.88%—substantially above the national average—which suggests elevated non-performing assets and warrants caution; while capital reserves are sufficient, this metric indicates higher-than-average risk relative to peer institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
